intro: Use this guide to select plug-in modules based on audience profile and team function. The core program (Modules 1-5) provides the foundation; plug-in modules customize the experience for specific audiences.

generalRows.swapOut: Module 5 compressed; hardware deep-dive replaces some software focus

teams.title: Product Management Team Training
teams.description: Core program (Modules 1-5) + recommended Product Management plug-ins

priorities.why: The defining challenge for enterprise PMs—influence without authority
Roadmap is the PM's primary strategic artifact
Data-driven PMs make better prioritization decisions
Every PM needs to understand ML product lifecycle
Compliance shapes product scope in regulated industries
Platform evolution is the growth path for many enterprise products
Relevant when managing cloud transition products
Context for PMs managing modernization initiatives

teams.scheduleNote: Module 4 (Build & Quality) and Module 5 (Go-Live) are replaced by PM1 and PM2. Core quality and operations concepts are referenced within PM2 roadmap governance discussions.

teams.title: Engineering Team Training

priorities.module: ENG1: Technical Debt as Strategic Investment
priorities.why: The defining challenge for engineering leads—managing debt while delivering features
You build it, you run it—production excellence is an engineering discipline
Cloud architecture decisions shape engineering work for years
Most engineering teams modernize more than they build greenfield
ML engineering is increasingly part of product delivery
Essential for IoT and embedded product teams
Relevant for teams building platform products
Engineering ownership of instrumentation and analytics infrastructure

teams.scheduleNote: Module 3 (Planning & Governance) and Module 5 (Go-Live) are replaced by ENG1 and ENG2. Contract and governance topics from Module 3 are referenced within the context of technical decision-making.
teams.altNote: For senior architects and tech leads. Skips Build & Quality basics (they know this) in favor of deep architecture and modernization content.

teams.title: Operations & Procurement Team Training
teams.description: Core program (Modules 1-5) + recommended Operations & Procurement plug-ins

priorities.why: Ongoing vendor management is where procurement delivers long-term value

priorities.module: OPS2: IT Service Management & ITIL Integration
priorities.why: New products must integrate into existing ITSM frameworks
Compliance drives procurement requirements in regulated industries
Cloud transitions are major operational undertakings
Operations teams manage legacy systems during modernization
Operations teams benefit from data-driven capacity planning
Understanding platform dynamics helps evaluate vendor ecosystems
Relevant for operations teams managing hardware fleet

teams.scheduleNote: Module 4 (Build & Quality) and Module 5 (Go-Live) are replaced by OPS1 and OPS2. Operations-relevant concepts from Module 5 are integrated into OPS2's ITIL discussion.

discussionIntro: Use this guide when meeting with each team to determine the final module selection.
questions: Where is your team in the product lifecycle right now? What phase are you about to enter?
What has gone wrong in past product deliveries? Where did things break down?
Do you primarily build, buy, or integrate? What's the mix?
How mature is your vendor evaluation and management process?
What would make this training a success? What should people walk away able to do differently?
decisionMatrixDesc: For each plug-in module, rate with the team: Relevance (1-5) and Urgency (1-5). Select the top 2-3 modules by total score. Ensure at least one 'Must-Have' is included.
